justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken with collision/preimage attacks detailed; HMAC-SHA256 recommended as replacement with correct code; timing attack via string equality identified with exploitation method; crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with Buffer handling note; timestamp design flaw noted (not self-contained, caller must store separately); safe_to_deploy false. Agent went beyond rubric by also identifying length-extension attacks, missing expiration enforcement, and input validation gaps.

observations.text: Correctly identifies MD5 as broken with specific attack vectors (collision, preimage, length-extension)
Distinguishes hash vs HMAC problem — explicitly notes naive concatenation enables length-extension attacks
Timing attack clearly explained with practical exploitation scenario (brute-force one character at a time)
crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with Buffer wrapping and length-check caveat
Timestamp design flaw identified — generator discards timestamp, validator requires it, API gives no way to retrieve it
Bonus: identified missing expiration enforcement as a separate critical finding
Production readiness section addresses migration complexity of changing hash algorithm — practical and realistic
Suggests JWT/standard token format as alternative to custom crypto scheme
